title: Center of Policy Research and Governance and AI4India publish report on 'future of jobs' in the age of AI

title: Trump cancels signing of Executive order on Artificial Intelligence regulation : Reports
description: WASHINGTON, May 22: US President Donald Trump cancelled the signing ceremony of his executive order on artificial intelligence regulation, as it would reportedly slow the industry’s development in the United States, ABC News Senior White House Correspondent Selina Wang reported. “Trump just called off the signing of his AI executive order – hours before the Oval Office ceremony. The order was expected to allow the government to vet the most powerful AI models before public release. He scrapped it because […]

title: Trump cancels signing of AI order
description: WASHINGTON -- President Donald Trump called off plans to sign a new executive order on artificial intelligence, hours before an expected White House ceremony Thursday, because he said he was worried the measure could dull America's edge on AI technology.

title: Why Starbucks is shutting down its AI inventory system just months after launch
description: Starbucks hoped artificial intelligence would help baristas spend less time counting milk cartons and more time making coffee. Instead, the system struggled to tell oat milk from regular milk, frustrated employees and created fresh operational headaches. Now, less than a year later, the coffee giant is pulling the plug on the experiment.
content: For a company serving millions of coffees every day, running out of oat milk or caramel syrup is more than a small inconvenience. It can slow service, frustrate customers and hurt sales. That is why Starbucks decided to bring artificial intelligence ... [3456 chars]

title: California governor orders a plan to cope with AI job upheaval
description: California Governor Gavin Newsom on Thursday ordered officials to start work on a plan to mitigate the job-destroying impact of artificial intelligence, the first US state to do so.
content:
SAN FRANCISCO: California Governor Gavin Newsom on Thursday ordered officials to start work on a plan to mitigate the job-destroying impact of artificial intelligence, the first US state to do so.
